title: Exhaustive queue reconciliation requires a discovery witness before classification

memory.observed: The projection receipt schema requires exact update-manifest entries and declares exhaustive enumeration as a runtime obligation, but a manifest containing only selected entries cannot itself prove that path discovery was complete.
memory.inferred: Discovery and semantic classification are separate epistemic steps. If they are collapsed, an implementation can silently omit a matching queue-update file and still produce a structurally valid manifest over the reduced set.
memory.proposed: Require an enumeration witness that records every matching path discovered within the declared commit horizon before any entry is classified as applied, superseded, invalid, or out of scope.
memory.superseded: A classified update manifest is sufficient evidence that all eligible update paths were discovered.
memory.unresolved: No authenticated runtime has yet generated the witness from complete repository history and demonstrated omission rejection against a deliberately hidden eligible path.

contradiction_resolution.apparent_conflict: The receipt can truthfully bind every entry in its manifest while the manifest is nevertheless incomplete relative to repository history.
contradiction_resolution.resolution: Byte-complete binding and path-complete discovery are different guarantees. The receipt proves the former only when paired with an independently verifiable enumeration witness proving the latter.
contradiction_resolution.selection_rule: Do not treat a reconciled queue projection as exhaustive unless every eligible path in the declared horizon appears in the discovery witness and every witnessed path has one explicit classification with a retained reason.

falsification_route: Generate a witness from an authenticated full-history checkout, then add or reveal one eligible queue-update path at or before the terminal commit that is absent from the witness; runtime verification must reject the witness and any dependent projection.
